_source: FreightClose engine configuration. Everything a non-engineer might change lives here: rate source, thresholds, calibration window, accounting accounts, and data-normalization maps.

rateSource._note: calibrated (default) prices on rates inferred from recent invoices; printed_override prices on the printed card (for a known rate change not yet in invoices). Divergence past threshold always flags.

calibration.recentWindowNote: April rates are calibrated from the most recent N months (Q1 2026) so the accrual reflects current rates, not a stale 6-month blend.

creditReserve.note: Books a small credit reserve equal to the historical net-adjustment run-rate (~-0.3% of invoiced) so the gross accrual is not systematically high vs eventual invoices.

accounting.rationale: Goods shipped in April; the carriers will invoice next month. The freight expense is recognized in the period the shipment occurred and carried as a matching accrued liability until the invoices arrive — a routine month-end accrued expense, not a loss contingency.
accounting.entryMemo: Accrue April 2026 outbound freight (FreightClose, calibrated estimate; reverses on receipt of carrier invoices)
accounting.outOfScope: Inventory freight capitalization (ASC 330) and shipping revenue (ASC 606) are out of scope — the package carries no inventory/COGS or customer-billing data.

normalization.serviceNote: No rate card prices by service level and invoices carry no service field; service is normalized for reporting only and the no-price-impact assumption is flagged.
